mandatory_claims: Zero-to-CAD synthesizes approximately one million executable CAD construction sequences
The framework uses a large language model (LLM) within a feedback-driven CAD environment
The synthesized CAD sequences cover a rich vocabulary of operations beyond sketch-and-extrude workflows
Fine-tuning a vision-language model on the synthetic data outperforms strong baselines, including GPT-5.2
mandatory_sections: Introduction to CAD and the problem of construction history
Agentic search problem and LLM integration
Synthesis and validation of CAD construction sequences
What would falsify this: cheapest experiment to invalidate the main claim
required_visuals: Examples of synthesized CAD construction sequences
Comparison chart of the performance of the vision-language model on synthetic data vs. baselines
likely_failures: Overemphasis on technical details without clear explanations
Lack of concrete examples and visual aids
Failure to provide alternative methods and cost comparisons
Insufficient discussion of limitations and potential falsification experiments
preventive_checks: Review for clarity and concision in technical explanations
Verify inclusion of concrete examples and visual aids
Check for alternative methods and cost comparisons in each section
Ensure discussion of limitations and potential falsification experiments
backup_remedies: Simplify technical explanations and add more visual aids
Add more concrete examples and case studies
Provide additional alternative methods and cost comparisons
Emphasize the importance of limitations and potential falsification experiments
reviewer_focus: Clarity and concision of technical explanations
Effectiveness of visual aids and examples
Thoroughness of alternative methods and cost comparisons
Sufficiency of discussion on limitations and potential falsification experiments
